scenarios.question: Which statement best describes Establishment of sleep?
options: wake cycle Social smile Drinking from cup Separation anxiety
This is an outdated concept no longer taught
This is not a recognized term in pediatric nursing
This only applies to adult patients, not children
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is wake cycle Social smile Drinking from cup Separation anxiety. Establishment of sleep: wake cycle Social smile Drinking from cup Separation anxiety (Slide 3)

scenarios.question: Which statement best describes Prevention?
options: This is an outdated concept no longer taught
This is not a recognized term in pediatric nursing
Parent Education Stress importance of periodic health checks
This only applies to adult patients, not children
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is This is an outdated concept no longer taught. Prevention: Parent Education Stress importance of periodic health checks (Slide 13)

options: Focus only on the child's physical symptoms
Exclude family members from care decisions
Use the same approach as with adult patients
must understand normal range for milestone achievement Establishment o
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is must understand normal range for milestone achievement Establishment o. must understand normal range for milestone achievement Establishment o. Family-centered, age-appropriate care is essential in pediatrics. (Slide 3)

scenarios.question: Which factor increases risk in pediatric patients?
options: of serious illness or even death
Factors only relevant in adults
Unrelated demographic information
Protective factors that reduce risk
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is of serious illness or even death. of serious illness or even death is a known risk factor. Immunizations Stress repeatedly the importance of immunizations and timing of ad (Slide 16)

scenarios.question: The anterior fontanelle typically closes by:
options: 12-18 months
2-3 years
3-6 months
Birth
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is 2-3 years. The anterior fontanelle (diamond-shaped) closes between 12-18 months. Posterior (triangle) closes by 2-3 months. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: Which reflex persists longest in infancy?
options: Babinski reflex (until 12 months)
Stepping reflex
Moro reflex
Rooting reflex
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is Rooting reflex. Babinski (toes fan out on sole stimulation) persists until ~12 months. Moro, rooting, stepping disappear by 4-6 months. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: An infant can sit unsupported by what age?
options: Birth
2-3 months
10-12 months
6-8 months
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is 2-3 months. Sitting unsupported: 6-8 months. Milestone delays may indicate motor delay. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: The Moro reflex is characterized by:
options: Extension and abduction of arms with crying, then adduction
Turning head toward a touch on the cheek
Fanning of toes on sole stroke
Sucking when the palate is touched
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is Sucking when the palate is touched. Moro (startle): sudden head extension causes arms to extend/abduct then adduct with crying. Present at birth, disappears 4-6 months. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: What is the normal heart rate range for an infant?
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is 160-200 bpm. Infant HR: 100-160 bpm resting. Tachycardia >160; bradycardia <100 (concerning). (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: When introducing solid foods, the nurse teaches parents to:
options: Introduce one food at a time and wait 3-5 days
Start with multiple foods immediately
Give only pureed meat first
Avoid all solids until 12 months
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is Start with multiple foods immediately. Introduce one new food every 3-5 days to identify allergies. Start with iron-fortified rice cereal at 4-6 months. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: An infant who can transfer objects hand-to-hand demonstrates which skill?
options: Fine motor development
Visual impairment
Cognitive delay
Gross motor development
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is Visual impairment. Transferring objects (6-7 months) shows developing pincer grasp and bilateral coordination. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: What is the recommended sleep position for infants?
options: Supine (back)
Any position
Prone (stomach)
Side-lying
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is Side-lying. Back to Sleep: supine only to reduce SIDS risk. Avoid soft bedding, pillows, bumpers. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: Which finding in an infant requires immediate attention?
options: Milia on the nose
Acrocyanosis of hands and feet
Cyanosis of lips and mucous membranes
Mongolian spot on the buttocks
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is Cyanosis of lips and mucous membranes. Central cyanosis (lips, tongue, trunk) indicates hypoxemia — emergency. Acrocyanosis (extremities) is normal in first 24-48h. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: An infant's first tooth typically erupts at:
options: Birth
6-8 months
12-14 months
18-24 months
scenarios.rationale: The correct answer is 6-8 months. Lower central incisors erupt first at 6-8 months. Full primary dentition (20 teeth) by 2.5 years. (Standard NCLEX)
